description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, angular profile and a coarse, uneven surface. Its coloration is predominantly gray, interspersed with patches of yellowish-brown and subtle hints of white, suggesting mineral inclusions or weathering effects. The stone's texture is rough and granular, with visible fractures and jagged edges that give it a natural, unprocessed appearance. Some faces are flatter and broader, while others are more pointed or sharply angled, indicating it may have broken off from a larger rock. The surface shows mottling and slight veining, with no signs of artificial shaping or smoothing. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, roadside verges, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, angular gray stone with yellowish-brown patches and a coarse, fractured surface. Its irregular shape and natural texture make it suitable for use as an urban obstacle or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view highlights a broad, slanted face with visible gray and yellowish-brown mottling and a sharp upper edge.
description_view_1: This angle shows a more triangular, pointed end with a darker, rougher surface and pronounced fractures.
description_view_2: This side reveals a flatter, wider face with a mix of gray and yellow tones and subtle veining.
description_view_3: This perspective displays a more tapered, elongated side with muted coloration and a less textured surface.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping, roadside verge, or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: irregular mottling and subtle veining with no artificial carvings; natural mineral inclusions create a patchy, organic pattern
attribute_weathering: rough and unpolished with visible fractures and minor surface erosion, indicating moderate exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well with vegetation and natural landscaping

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
